feat.critical_failure: As failure, but the target is slowed 2 instead of slowed 1.
feat.failure: The target is slowed 1 until the start of your next turn as it grabs for the pearls, and if it doesn't have a free hand (or similar appendage), it must spend its first action on its next turn to Release one item it's holding.
feat.name: Tears of Pearl
feat.success: The target is slowed 1 until the start of your next turn as it covets the pearls or reaches for them.
feat.text: You weep, and your tears become gleaming pearls as they fall to the ground, so beautiful others can't help but grab at them. All creatures adjacent to you must attempt a Will save against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. The pearls dissolve at the start of your next turn, turning into salt water.

traits.name: Emotion
traits.text: This effect alters a creature’s emotions. Effects with this trait always have the mental trait as well. Creatures with special training or that have mechanical or artificial intelligence are immune to emotion effects.

traits.name: Manipulate
traits.text: You must physically manipulate an item or make gestures to use an action with this trait. Creatures without a suitable appendage can’t perform actions with this trait. Manipulate actions often trigger reactions.

traits.name: Mental
traits.text: A mental effect can alter the target’s mind. It has no effect on an object or a mindless creature.

traits.name: Merfolk
traits.text: These aquatic humanoids have an upper body similar to a human and a lower body similar to a fish.

traits.name: Primal
traits.text:
This magic comes from the primal tradition, connecting to the natural world and instinct. Anything with this trait is magical.  
  
A creature with this trait is primarily constituted of or has a strong connection to primal magic.

traits.name: Visual
traits.text: A visual effect can affect only creatures that can see it. This applies only to visible parts of the effect, as determined by the GM.
